Node.description: Range of multiple VLANs for fine grained configuration

PropertyString.name: name
PropertyString.description: The unique name by which a range can be referred from other forms. When a range is created this field has an automatically generated value and can be changed by user. Currently a VLAN range can be referred by Emulated Router and IP ranges.

PropertyInt.name: firstId
PropertyInt.description: The first ID to be used for the outer VLAN tag.

PropertyInt.name: incrementStep
PropertyInt.description: Frequency of outer VLAN ID increment. E.g., value of 10 means increment VLAN ID once for every 10 IP addresse.

PropertyInt.name: increment
PropertyInt.description: Amount of increment per increment step for outer VLAN. E.g. increment step = 10 and increment = 2 means increment VLAN ID by 2 for every 10 Ips

PropertyInt.name: uniqueCount
PropertyInt.description: Number of unique outer VLAN IDs to use.

PropertyInt.name: priority
PropertyInt.description: The 802.1Q priority to be used for the outer VLAN tag.

PropertyInt.name: innerFirstId
PropertyInt.description: The first ID to be used for the inner VLAN tag.

PropertyInt.name: innerIncrementStep
PropertyInt.description: Frequency of inner VLAN ID increment. E.g., value of 10 means increment VLAN ID once for every 10 IP addresse.

PropertyInt.name: innerIncrement
PropertyInt.description: Amount of increment per increment step for Inner VLAN. E.g. increment step = 10 and increment = 2 means increment VLAN ID by 2 for every 10 Ips

PropertyInt.name: innerUniqueCount
PropertyInt.description: Number of unique inner VLAN IDs to use.

PropertyInt.name: innerPriority
PropertyInt.description: The 802.1Q priority to be used for the inner VLAN tag.

PropertyInt.name: idIncrMode
PropertyInt.description: Method used to increment VLAN IDs. May take the following values: 0 (Outer VLAN first), 1 (Inner VLAN first), 2 (Both).

PropertyString.name: etherType
PropertyString.description: The Ethernet Type value in the VLAN Tag.
